semantic.role: Defines Java-specific AST extraction patterns for ArchSpine's code analysis pipeline.
responsibilities: Specifies pattern-based rules for identifying Java import statements.
Defines how to recognize Java class and interface definitions (including public variants).
Describes usage detection patterns for method calls and object instantiation.
Provides the foundation for automatic extraction of structural metadata from Java source files.
outOfScope: Does not cover runtime behavior or execution semantics.
Does not handle languages other than Java.
Does not include dependency resolution or module-level analysis.
Excludes documentation generation or rendering logic.

English.purpose: This document defines the pattern rules that ArchSpine uses to parse Java source code. It tells the system how to recognize imports, class/interface declarations, and typical usage patterns like method calls and object creation. Without this file, ArchSpine would not know how to extract structural information from Java code.
English.context_and_audience: This file is intended for developers maintaining ArchSpine's multi-language analysis capabilities and for AI agents that need to understand how the system processes Java code. It is part of the AST rules layer and should be modified only when the Java pattern definitions need to be updated, for example to support new language constructs or changes in Java syntax.
key_takeaways: Imports are matched using a simple 'import $SOURCE;' pattern.
Classes and interfaces are captured via pattern templates that recognize both public and non-public declarations.
Usage patterns detect method calls ($OBJ.$NAME($$$ARGS)) and object instantiation (new $NAME($$$ARGS)).
These patterns are language-specific and are used by the AST extractor to build a structured index of a Java codebase.
